content: Join my mailing list Previous Next

Lately, George had been having trouble sleeping. He had taken to getting out of bed in the middle of the night and walking around to tire himself. He always took great care to not wake his wife, waiting for her to be immersed in the furthest depths of sleep before slowly slipping out of bed, planting a delicate kiss on her forehead, and leaving the room to traipse about the house in the dark. It was on such a late night walk, in the height of the witching hour, that he noticed movement in the corner of his eye, outside of his kitchen window. He drew closer, and began to make out a human-like figure moving in the lit window of his neighbor’s house.
It appeared to be the silhouette of a person moving rhythmically, in tempo with some unheard melody. The dancer was lanky but otherwise featureless. He wondered why his neighbor was up at this hour, and what possessed him to do this in the dead of night. Perhaps he too found no solace in his bed, and this was a bizarre exercise for inciting fatigue. This was not quite right, though. This figure’s frame did not match that of his neighbor. George hadn’t seen any visitors come through that day, either. The dance seemed completely random at first, but after a while he was able to identify three distinct phases in the choreography. The first phase was reminiscent of a marionette doll being hoisted and then suddenly released, with the head and arms raising up and then abruptly falling down, limp. The dancer would continue this, swaying from left to right, while introducing a new motion; it raised one hand up to its head, seemingly caressing one side of its face before sweeping the hand down its body. It repeated this on each side. Finally, the dancer would sharply fall forward, and would slowly rise, visibly trembling and nodding its head. These three phases would loop in a cycle, with various bodily tremors and gestures interspersed. George stood in place, transfixed by the dancer’s erratic and esoteric movements. He noticed a lump developing in his throat, and felt a creeping anxiety that the strange figure would stop dancing and notice him staring through the window.
As George’s heart began to drum faster, he noticed the dancer’s pace quicken as well. This new tempo made the dance even more uncanny, which disconcerted him further. Eventually, the silhouette danced with an almost violent intensity, in time with the pounding in George’s chest. Though he had the extreme desire to retreat to his room, where his wife still slept soundly, George found himself unable to move his legs, turn his head, or even close his eyes. He continued watching for another minute. Without warning, the dancer stood straight and perfectly still. The perfect darkness of its silhouette was suddenly broken by two bright, white eyes in its head. Though George was many yards away, he could somehow make out the fine details of the eyes, from the stark whites to the dark, constricted pupils. He could tell that the figure’s eyes were trained directly on him, now watching him obsessively as he had watched it before.
Startled, George gasped and took a step back, finally able to move. As he tried to collect his senses, he noticed that he was having difficulty closing his mouth. His jaw was hinged open from his previous surprise and sharp intake of air, and try as he might, he could not make it shut. In his efforts to move his jaw around and regain full muscular control, he found himself opening his mouth wider, unable to hinge it close. As his confusion descended into alarm, and alarm into panic, he involuntarily began opening his mouth even wider while trying to close it, until he was completely agape. His mouth was open to the point of causing pain. His lower jaw was so far hinged open that it pressed against his throat, making it difficult to breathe. And still he could not swing it shut, and still the dancer’s eyes were fixed squarely on him.
George was now frantic, in the throes of hysteria. He tried crying out for his wife, but in his current state he could only muster grunts and unintelligible bellows, too muffled for her to hear. In the midst of his growing frenzy, he tripped and tumbled backward. He lay on the kitchen floor, laboriously hyperventilating, weeping and cradling his face, alone except for the dark figure observing him from across the yard. He lay like this for a few moments, hidden in the darkness from all eyes except those of the dancer. He stared back at his only companion, and suddenly realized that the dancer appeared to be growing larger – no, it was getting closer. Unbound by walls and by the forces of the Earth, the dark thing slowly approached George, gliding through space. Now focused on the figure’s movement, he felt panic slowly leave him. His heart was no longer pounding in his chest – no, in fact it was quite still, and despite his circumstances he felt the growing urge to fall asleep. The figure had now passed through the window of the kitchen and was almost hovering above him. Best to sleep here, George thought. I think my jaw should fix itself if I get some rest. Mary will be alarmed to see me this way in the morning but I’ll apologize and we’ll hold each other and then we’ll make coffee and talk about what happened. But now I need to sleep. The figure now completely enveloped George. His eyes closed, but in his eyelids he could still see the bright eyes of the dancer. The eyes moved closer and closer and for an instant the whites completely filled the field of his vision. Finally, George knew peace.
summary: A short story about being unable to fall asleep
title: Dancer

content:
It’s time to pull it back on OTUS ("_ Of the United States") acronyms. We’ve simply gone too far.
POTUS - I’m okay with this. Has a faux-Latin feel to it, and western politicians do like to cosplay Roman Republic.
FLOTUS - Does have an elegant, floral ring to it. When we have a First Gentleman it will become dangerously close to a homophobic slur.
SCOTUS - Sounds like a venereal disease.
VPOTUS - This is just obscene, completely aura-less. Somehow worse than “Veep.”
BRUTUS - This is not an acronym, this is the Roman Senator that betrayed Julius Caesar.
COTUS - This one stands for the Constitution of the United States. Obvious coitus joke aside, this is the syndrome that results from untreated SCOTUS.
SLOTUS - You’re just messing with me now.
summary: An exercise in absurd acrostic pedantry
title: OTUS Acronyms are out of hand

content:
For all inquiries, you can email me at vinnyseeram@gmail.com.
If you’d like to receive email updates for upcoming shows or blog content, you can join my mailing list using the form below.
title: Contact Me

content:
Vinny Seeram is a stand-up comedian based in New York City. His draws from his experiences growing up as a second generation Indo-Guyanese immigrant in the Bronx, and his observations on politics, race, and culture.
Vinny performs all over New York, most frequently at his home club Q.E.D. Astoria, where he hosts a weekly open mic and his comedy showcase show, vinnydotcomedy. He has also been featured in festivals across the United States, including:
Boston Comedy Festival (Boston, MA) Vice City Comedy Festival (Miami, FL) Mountain Fresh Comedy Festival (Colorado) Crooked Mouth Comedy Festival (Endicott, NY) Comedy Mob Festival (New York, NY), Semi-finalist HaHa Harvest Comedy Festival (Jersey City, NJ) Salem Comedy and Spirits Festival (Salem, MA) Last Word Comedy Festival (Williamsburg, VA) Vinny has also appeared as an actor in several theatrical productions in New York. In AfterWork Theater’s productions of You Can’t Take it With You and The Crucible he played Tony Kirby and John Proctor, respectively. He has most recently appeared in ScriptClub NYC’s production of The 39 Steps as The Professor and other supporting roles.
title: About
